patch.value: Telemetry worker: no Postgres reconnect — ingestion stalls with events stuck in RabbitMQ after postgres restart

Incident 2026-06-09/10: pm-telemetry-postgres restarted; pm-telemetry-worker kept its dead connection and failed every insert with 'worker_insert_failed: the connection is closed' for ~24h. 15,778 events backed up in pm.telemetry.events (RabbitMQ retained all — no data loss); Postgres ingested only 11 events/24h. Manual 'docker restart pm-telemetry-worker' reconnected and drained the full backlog in ~2 min (24h count restored to 15,731). Fix needed in the worker (remote stack, scripts/prod/telemetry host): (1) reconnect/retry on closed Postgres connection instead of failing forever, (2) container healthcheck that fails on repeated insert errors so docker restarts it automatically, (3) optional: stack-health script should WARN when queue depth high while 24h ingest count is near zero.

patch.value: Telemetry worker reconnects to Postgres with backoff after a restart; queued RabbitMQ events drain automatically; runbook documents the recovery path.

## Context
Incident 2026-06-09/10 on the remote telemetry stack: pm-telemetry-postgres restarted; pm-telemetry-worker kept the dead connection and failed every insert ('worker_insert_failed: the connection is closed') for ~24h. RabbitMQ retained all 15,778 backed-up events (no data loss); manual `docker restart pm-telemetry-worker` drained the backlog in ~2 min.

## Fix (remote stack, scripts/prod/telemetry on the host — NOT in this repo's tracked files)
1. Worker reconnect/retry with backoff on closed Postgres connection instead of failing forever.
2. Container healthcheck failing on repeated insert errors so docker auto-restarts.
3. Optional alert when queue depth grows abnormally (Grafana panel already exists).
Standing rule: never write host IPs/tokens into tracked files; verify via scripts/prod/telemetry/check-stack-health.sh.

## Definition of done
Kill-postgres drill: worker reconnects unaided and drains the queue; healthcheck restarts a wedged worker; drill evidence recorded here (counts before/after).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Second live occurrence 2026-07-12: pm-telemetry-postgres restarted ~04:00 (host maintenance window; container Up 8h vs worker Up 8d), worker logged continuous worker_insert_failed:the connection is closed, pm.telemetry.events backlogged to 111,382 messages / 1 consumer while Postgres kept serving reads. Remediation applied: docker compose restart telemetry-worker → consumer reattached, queue draining. This defect now has a repeatable operational cost on every Postgres restart — reconnect-with-backoff in the worker insert path remains the fix; consider raising priority.
